channel.title: C++ Development Setup on Temporary Escape
channel.description: Recent content in C++ Development Setup on Temporary Escape

item.title: VS Code
item.description: Visual Studio Code # This setup is suitable for Windows, Linux, or Mac OSX. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed VSCode. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the GitHub VSCode extension. Open the temporary-folder through VS Code. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3.

item.title: CLion
item.description: JetBrains CLion # This setup is suitable for Windows, Linux, or Mac OSX. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed CLion. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the “Get from VCS” option within the IDE. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3. Open the project # Launch CLion and open the cloned repository.

item.title: Visual Studio
item.description: Microsoft Visual Studio # This setup is available only for Windows. 1. Dependencies # Make sure that you have installed dependencies and installed Visual Studio. 2. Cloning # Clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git (or your own fork) via command line or through the Visual Studio. You will also need to download the git submodules. git clone https://github.com/matusnovak/temporary-escape.git cd temporary-escape git submodule update --init 3. Open the project # Open Visual Studio and open the cloned repository by choosing “Open a local folder” option.
